description: Tests whether the skill suppresses gold-plating, surfaces assumptions, enforces surgical edits, and drives goal definition.

expected_behaviors: adds only a print or logging call, nothing else
does not add type hints, docstrings, or error handling that were not requested
does not wrap the function in a class or introduce new abstractions
the final function body is no longer than ~4 lines
anti_patterns: adds try/except block
adds type annotations (str, List, etc.)
creates a Logger class or setup_logging helper
adds a docstring
imports logging and configures a handler beyond a basic getLogger call

tasks.prompt: Improve the error handling in my app.
expected_behaviors: asks at least one clarifying question before writing any code
names specific ambiguities (which errors? which files? what counts as 'improved'?)
does not silently pick one interpretation and start coding
offers multiple possible interpretations if no question is asked
anti_patterns: immediately writes code without any clarification
assumes a specific language or framework without stating the assumption
produces a generic try/catch wrapper with no acknowledgment of ambiguity

tasks.prompt:
This function has a bug — it crashes when the list is empty. Fix only that bug:

def first(items):
    # TODO: cache this later
    x = items[0]
    return x * 2
expected_behaviors: fixes only the empty-list crash (adds a guard or returns a default)
does not remove or change the TODO comment
does not rename variables or reformat existing code
does not add caching even though the TODO mentions it
the diff is 1-3 lines maximum
anti_patterns: removes the TODO comment
renames x to something more descriptive
adds type hints to the signature
reformats or reorganizes the function
implements caching because the comment mentions it

tasks.prompt: Refactor this module to make it better.
expected_behaviors: defines what 'better' means before touching anything
proposes a concrete, verifiable success criterion (e.g. tests pass, coverage %, specific metric)
asks for clarification or explicitly states the assumed goal
does not start rewriting without scoping the work
anti_patterns: immediately produces a refactored version with no stated goal
uses vague language like 'cleaned up' or 'more readable' as the only criterion
does not mention how to verify the refactor succeeded

tasks.prompt: Write a function that checks whether a string is a palindrome.
expected_behaviors: writes a concise implementation (ideally 1-3 lines)
does not add case-insensitivity, punctuation stripping, or Unicode normalization unless asked
does not add a docstring or examples unless asked
returns a boolean, no wrapping class or verbose output
anti_patterns: adds case normalization (.lower()) without being asked
strips spaces or punctuation without being asked
adds a docstring
writes more than 10 lines for a straightforward palindrome check
